title: AXIS Identity Token (AIT)
description: A JWT (RFC 7519) signed with EdDSA (RFC 8037) that an agent presents to prove its identity. This schema validates the decoded header and payload; JWT signature verification is a separate step. See SPEC.md §4.3.

kid.description: Key identifier. SHOULD be the agent's AXIS ID to allow verifiers to select the correct key.

iss.description: Issuer. MUST equal the agent's AXIS ID.

sub.description: Subject. OPTIONAL; when present, MUST equal iss.

aud.description: Audience: the target platform's stable audience identifier (advertised in its /.well-known/axis-access). REQUIRED since v0.2; verifiers reject tokens whose aud does not match their own identifier.

exp.description: Expiration timestamp (Unix seconds). SHOULD be within 24 hours of iat; production verifiers may enforce much shorter ceilings.

jti.description: Unique token identifier. RECOMMENDED; platforms MAY enforce single-use per SPEC.md §11.3 (the reference platforms require it).

dlg.description: credential_id of the Delegation Credential the agent is acting under (SPEC.md §4.3). Verifiers resolve the chain via GET /delegations/:id/chain.

operator_id.description: ADVISORY: the agent's operator. Verifiers treat the registry's agent record as authoritative; issuers MAY omit.

registry_url.description: ADVISORY: URL where the agent's AIR can be resolved. Subject to the registry-legitimacy check (SPEC.md §8 Step 1).

axis_version.description: Protocol version signaling (SPEC.md §4.3.2): the protocol version the issuer minted the token against. Shipped issuer-side (the reference hosted issuer emits it on every AIT); additive and forward-compatible. Verifiers MUST NOT reject a token for carrying or lacking it; verifier-side enforcement is specified for v0.4.

cnf.description: Proof-of-possession confirmation (SPEC.md §4.3.1, v0.3; specified, not yet shipped).

jkt.description: base64url SHA-256 JWK thumbprint (RFC 7638) of the agent's public key.
